channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A record number of political parties register for Haiti's first election in a decade
item.description: A record 280 political parties had registered by Thursday's deadline to participate in Haiti's first general election in a decade, hopeful for a chance to help ease their country's multiple crises.
item.content:encoded:
A record 280 political parties had registered by Thursday's deadline to participate in Haiti's first general election in a decade, hopeful for a chance to help ease their country's multiple crises.

(Image credit: Odelyn Joseph)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China slams Trump's trade investigation, as it approves a 5-year economic plan
item.description: China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.
item.content:encoded:
China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: Despite Trump's frustration, Spain's Pedro Sánchez doubles down on opposing the war
item.description: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Miguel Macias

item.title: Khameni's killing raises old questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
item.description: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.
item.content:encoded: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Fear of Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz could further slow the flow of oil
item.description: Attacks by Iran have already nearly halted the flow of oil through the vital waterway as commercial ship crews fear being hit by missiles, drones or mines.
item.content:encoded:
Attacks by Iran have already nearly halted the flow of oil through the vital waterway as commercial ship crews fear being hit by missiles, drones or mines.

(Image credit: Handout/Royal Thai Navy)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: U.S. investigates strike on Iranian school as the war sparks a global oil crisis
item.description: Trump, who promised to lower gas prices, is tapping the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as war drives prices up. And, the U.S. investigates the strike on an Iranian school that killed at least 165 people.
item.content:encoded:
Trump, who promised to lower gas prices, is tapping the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as war drives prices up. And, the U.S. investigates the strike on an Iranian school that killed at least 165 people.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz as U.S.-Israel strikes continue
item.description: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Israel continue airstrikes in the country, triggering what's being described as the "largest oil disruption in history."
item.content:encoded: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Israel continue airstrikes in the country, triggering what's being described as the "largest oil disruption in history."
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Iran says its new leader made his 1st address, vowing to keep Strait of Hormuz closed
item.description: Iran's state media issued what it said was a statement by Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, vowing to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and keep up attacks on U.S. bases in the region.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's state media issued what it said was a statement by Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, vowing to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and keep up attacks on U.S. bases in the region.

(Image credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S.-Israel strikes continue as Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz, Trump ran on lower gas prices, but the Iran war challenges that, probe points to U.S. being at fault for Iran school strike.
item.content:encoded: U.S.-Israel strikes continue as Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz, Trump ran on lower gas prices, but the Iran war challenges that, probe points to U.S. being at fault for Iran school strike.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: To stop Africa from hemorrhaging local talent, scientist creates drug discovery lab
item.description: A Zambian scientist is on a quest to prevent brain drain from Africa so he's established a state-of-the-art drug discovery lab in South Africa.
item.content:encoded: A Zambian scientist is on a quest to prevent brain drain from Africa so he's established a state-of-the-art drug discovery lab in South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
